items.title: Peru Presidential candidates make final pitch in tight, crime-driven race
items.summary: Conservative candidate Keiko Fujimori and leftist rival Roberto Sanchez made their final appeals to voters ahead of Presidential runoff election on June 7

items.title: Thailand to appoint conciliators for UN-backed mediation with Cambodia, says Foreign Minister
items.summary: Cambodia said ​it had launched a compulsory conciliation ⁠process under the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea

items.title: Seven killed as Israel strikes in south Lebanon’s Tyre: civil defence
items.summary: Israeli military issued new evacuation warnings for nine southern Lebanese towns and villages, with Lebanon’s state-run National News Agency reporting people were fleeing

items.title: Israeli fire kills at least 18 in Gaza, U.S. envoy visits hostage family protest
items.summary: The danger facing aid seekers in Gaza has compounded what international hunger experts this week called a “worst-case scenario of famine” in the besieged enclave.

items.title: Vladimir Putin pledges non-interference in ‘delicate’ India-China ties; backs Modi, Xi to resolve border issues amicably
items.summary: India and China rolled  out a series of measures in the last over one year to rebuild their relationship after it came under severe strain following the deadly Galwan Valley clashes in 2020

items.title: First privately developed U.S. nuclear reactor in over 40 years achieves first criticality point
items.summary: The Department of Energy described the milestone, achieved on June 4, as "rebirth of America’s nuclear industry" and a "historic moment" for U.S. energy

items.title: Watch: Is the Israel–U.S. alliance straining over Iran?
items.summary: Tensions appear to be emerging within the Israel–U.S. alliance as differences grow over how to deal with Iran. While Washington seems increasingly open to diplomatic engagement and a potential agreement, Israel continues to advocate a tougher, security-first approach amid escalating regional tensions.In this video, we examine the latest developments in Lebanon, President Trump’s dilemma, the UAE’s role in regional diplomacy, and more.

items.title: Israel-Iran war LIVE: Seven killed as Israel strikes in south Lebanon’s Tyre
items.summary: Hezbollah has rejected the Israel-Lebanon talks, while a previous ceasefire announced on April 17 has been breached daily, with Israeli troops deployed deep inside Lebanese territory and the Iran-backed militants continuing to attack Israel

items.title: Senate passes $70 billion immigration enforcement bill without limits on Trump settlement fund
items.summary: Senators voted 52-47 for the $70 billion legislation to fund Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol for the next three years, through the end of Mr. Trump's term

items.title: Watch: U.S.- Iran war: Peace talks stall after Hezbollah rejects truce
items.summary: A proposed Lebanon-Israel truce faces uncertainty after Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem rejected the conditional ceasefire announced by Lebanese and Israeli envoys. Hezbollah is demanding a comprehensive ceasefire and a full Israeli withdrawal, while warning of renewed attacks on northern Israel.

items.title: China ready to work with Myanmar to maintain border stability: Wang Yi

items.title: Pakistan ‘forever grateful’ to Trump for India ceasefire intervention: PM Sharif
items.summary: At an event marking the 250th anniversary of American independence, Shehbaz Sharif described Pakistan-U.S. ties as a "true and special relationship" spanning nearly eight decades

items.title: UN nuclear watchdog says it’s been unable to inspect Iranian facilities
items.summary: The  International Atomic Energy Agency warned that it was “unable to discharge its safeguards responsibilities” that it has under the Safeguards Agreement of the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty

items.title: Gunmen kidnap seven students from school in northwestern Nigeria
items.summary: One student escaped and was in custody; the police said it wasn't clear where the students were taken but efforts were under way to rescue the remaining six

items.summary: In a televised ⁠broadcast, Sultan Hassanal said he would continue to hold the key roles of Prime Minister, Defence Minister, and Finance Minister

items.title: U.S. House passes Bill to aid Ukraine and impose new sanctions on Russia
items.summary: The 226-195 vote is a sign of impatience with President Donald Trump’s approach to the war and represents the House’s second major foreign policy break with Mr. Trump this week

items.title: India among Russia's top foreign trade partners: PM Mishustin
items.summary: In the financial year 2024–25, total India-Russia trade reached an all-time high of about $68.7 billion, up sharply from roughly $13 billion in 2021, marking nearly a five - to six- fold increase over four years.

items.title: In open letter to Putin, Zelenskyy calls for meeting and ceasefire
items.summary: Ukraine proposes ending this war through direct engagement between us — and you. I am proposing a meeting, says Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy

items.title: U.S. imposes sanctions on Cuban President, Treasury website shows

items.title: Trump announces $700 million funding for coal plants, energy infrastructure

items.title: How ISWAP emerged as the dominant jihadist force in Africa’s Lake Chad basin
items.summary: The Islamic State West Africa Province steadily eclipsed Boko Haram to become the most powerful militant organisation in the region; with a structured command system and strong territorial presence, what began as a local insurgency in Nigeria is now one of Africa’s major transnational security threats

items.title: Five Eyes alliance warns that China is targeting personnel via fake job ads
items.summary: The alliance said Chinese agents pretending to be human resources consultants for legitimate-looking companies use websites such as LinkedIn, Indeed and Upwork to post job ads

items.title: Sudan, DR Congo top world's most neglected crises: Norway aid group
items.summary: The NGO's list is based on three criteria: lack of humanitarian funding, lack of media coverage, and lack of political will within the international community

items.title: Mali junta bans motorcycles outside cities and creates military zones to reduce militant attacks
items.summary: Motorcycles are the primary means of transportation in rural areas of Mali and are used by rural residents and armed groups

items.title: Trump slams ‘unpatriotic’ US House vote to end Iran war

items.title: Nepal Foreign Minister to visit India from June 5

items.title: Hezbollah rejects latest ceasefire agreement as Israeli strikes kill 4 in Lebanon
items.summary: Hezbollah leader Naim Kassem, in a written statement, said the agreement's demand that Hezbollah fighters leave southern Lebanon under fire would mean “surrender, defeat and achieving the enemy's goals”

items.title: Bangladesh says it foiled multiple attempts by India to force people into the country
items.summary: Dhaka has ⁠repeatedly said anyone identified as a Bangladeshi national should be returned through ⁠formal legal and diplomatic channels rather than being driven across the border

items.title: AI to double data centre power and water consumption by 2030, UN researchers say
items.summary: “The ‌public debate still often treats AI as software, but AI is also physical infrastructure: data centres, electricity generation, cooling systems, transmission networks, chips, minerals, land and water,” said Kaveh ‌Madani, the institute’s director and the report’s lead author.

items.title: Pakistan accuses India of weaponising water after treaty suspension
items.summary: Pakistan's Foreign Ministry spokesperson Tahir Andrabi said that New Delhi had not consulted Islamabad on the two Chenab River projects that he said would undermine the Indus Water Treaty

items.title: Iranian-French cartoonist and filmmaker Marjane Satrapi dies at 56
items.summary: President Emmanuel Macron and his wife Brigitte Macron “pay tribute to a remarkable artist who transformed an Iranian childhood into a universal fable,” a statement said.

items.title: Nine killed in overnight strikes in Gaza, says hospital
items.summary: The nine people were killed in at least four separate strikes in Gaza city, according to Shifa Hospital, which received the bodies

items.title: Brazil takes a historic HDI leap with focused planning and public spending
items.summary: UN report reveals the country’s cash-transfer scheme has played a key role in raising its Human Development Index score from 0.744 in 2012 to 0.805 in 2024, on a scale from 0 to 1; as Brazil enters election cycle, the far-right narrative that the scheme discourages people from working draws backlash

items.title: Israel, Lebanon agree to conditional ceasefire
items.summary: Israel and Lebanon, which do not have formal diplomatic relations, also agreed to create “pilot zones” in which the Lebanese armed forces “will take exclusive control of the territory to the exclusion of all non-state actors”

items.title: Iran's Foreign Minister warns any attack on Beirut will trigger 'full-scale resumption of war'
items.summary: Iran has repeatedly insisted that any deal to end the wider West Asia war — which its ally Hezbollah joined on March 2 — must also halt the fighting in Lebanon.

items.title: West Asia war highlights: Trump says Iran talks could yield result this weekend
items.summary: "I hear the negotiation itself is going very well, actually," Mr. Trump told reporters, adding of a potential deal, "It could happen... over the weekend."

items.title: Rubio warns of Ukraine war 'escalation' risk
items.summary: The remarks came after Ukrainian drones struck energy infrastructure in Russia's second-largest city, St. Petersburg, an attack Kyiv called retaliation for a mass wave of Russian strikes on Tuesday that killed at least 23 people.

items.title: SpaceX’s IPO is set to be the biggest ever and could make Elon Musk a trillionaire
items.summary: SpaceX says it plans to raise up to $75 billion when it goes public this month, setting the stage for the largest-ever stock market debut and putting Elon Musk on course to becoming the world’s first trillionaire

items.title: U.S. proposes 12.5% tariff on India and other countries, Indian govt says it ‘remains engaged’ with U.S.
items.summary: The action follows investigations launched against 60 countries over what the USTR described as their failure to impose and effectively enforce bans on imports made with forced labour

items.title: U.S. sanctions alleged rebel commanders in DR Congo
items.summary: The sanctions target Gustave Kubwayo, alleged to be the commander of FDLR, and John Imani Nzenze, alleged to be the March 23 (M23) commander and chief of intelligence, says the U.S. Treasury in a statement

items.title: Brazil moves to limit abortion access for child rape victims
items.summary: Under the overturned regulation, minors who disagreed with their parents or guardians on their pregnancy could obtain free legal assistance to safeguard their rights, including getting an abortion

items.title: North Korea slams U.S. remarks comparing South to 'dagger'
items.summary: The comments reflected how Washington intended to use South Korea "as an important geopolitical instrument to realise its regional strategy aimed at containing China," North Korea said
